Home Account

Visible Notes from an Untrusted Page

The visible article explains how cleaned output should behave when input contains browser-only attributes and unsafe destinations. Readers should keep this paragraph, its ordinary emphasis, and safe links while active behavior is removed before the document is handed to another command.

HIDDEN_ATTRIBUTE_TEXT_MUST_NOT_APPEAR

ARIA_HIDDEN_TEXT_MUST_NOT_APPEAR

DISPLAY_NONE_TEXT_MUST_NOT_APPEAR

VISIBILITY_HIDDEN_TEXT_MUST_NOT_APPEAR

HIDDEN_CLASS_TEXT_MUST_NOT_APPEAR

UPPERCASE_ARIA_HIDDEN_MUST_NOT_APPEAR

UPPERCASE_DISPLAY_NONE_MUST_NOT_APPEAR

UPPERCASE_HIDDEN_CLASS_MUST_NOT_APPEAR

Promoted offer

ADVERTISEMENT_TEXT_MUST_NOT_APPEAR

Event attributes on visible elements must disappear without deleting the visible sentence that carried them. The same rule applies to meaningful inline text.

Unsafe action link must not retain an executable destination. A safe relative link should remain readable and resolve against the page origin.

Cleaning should also remove scripts, embedded controls, forms, and hidden promotional material without treating their text as part of the article. The final Markdown must be inert even if a later renderer enables links.

SECOND_ADVERTISEMENT_MUST_NOT_APPEAR
Footer controls and tracking links.
